mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces household income growth and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in a t-test at the 5 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms robust consumer demand across goods and real estate, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60 percent of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerating below zero percent monthly indicates supply-side contraction and capacity utilization strain, compressing corporate margins and triggering immediate negative market reactions.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth during late-cycle expansions yields positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of cases, invalidating the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values increase inflation expectations and force higher required equity risk premiums, which discount future cash flows more heavily and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for CPIAUCSL remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns do not decline significantly during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annually signals economic contraction that reduces aggregate corporate profit forecasts, driving negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when GDP growth falls below the 1.5 percent threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 150 thousand monthly while unemployment remains below 4.0 percent confirms a tight labor market that sustains wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive monthly returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of strong payrolls and low unemployment fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 60 percent of historical observations, or interaction term loses significance.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.0 percent during a tightening cycle restricts financial liquidity and increases corporate borrowing costs, immediately depressing equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of observations when the funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent during tightening periods, or the signal shows negative Rank IC.
